prompt: Explain the definition of compactness in your own words.
learner_response: A set K is compact if every open cover of K has a finite subcover. That is, for any collection of open sets covering K, we can find finitely many that still cover K.
notes: Complete correct answer with all required terms

prompt: Using the formal definition explain why (0 1) is not compact.
learner_response: (0,1)은 닫혀 있지 않아서 compact하지 않습니다.

prompt: Using the formal definition explain why (0 1) is not compact.
learner_response: 열린 덮개 {(1/n, 1) : n=2,3,...}를 잡으면, 각 점 x에 대해 n > 1/x인 구간이 존재한다. 하지만 유한 개를 택하면 N=max(n_i)로 놓을 때 1/(N+1)이 덮이지 않으므로 유한 부분덮개가 불가능하다.
notes: Correct open cover argument with all required terms

prompt: Using the formal definition explain why (0 1) is not compact.

prompt: Outline the Heine-Borel proof structure using definition and (0 1).
learner_response: Heine-Borel: compact iff closed and bounded in R^n. (→) compact이면 closed: 극한점 논증. compact이면 bounded: B(0,n)으로 덮어 유한 부분덮개 추출. (←) closed and bounded이면 compact: nested intervals로 bisection하여 증명.
notes: Complete proof outline with both directions

prompt: Outline the Heine-Borel proof structure using definition and (0 1).

notes: Knows the theorem but not the proof structure

prompt: Outline the Heine-Borel proof structure using definition and (0 1).
learner_response: compact면 closed이다. 그리고 bounded이다. 반대 방향은 모르겠다.

prompt: Explain the proof structure of Heine-Borel.
learner_response: Heine-Borel: closed and bounded implies compact in R^n. 증명은 Bolzano-Weierstrass를 사용하여 nested intervals 논증을 한다. 열린 덮개를 잡고, 구간을 이등분하면서 유한 부분덮개가 불가능한 쪽을 택한다.
notes: Correct proof sketch

prompt: Explain the proof structure of Heine-Borel.

prompt: Using the formal definition explain why (0 1) is not compact.

prompt: Explain the definition of compactness in your own words.
learner_response: A set is compact if it is sequentially compact, meaning every sequence has a convergent subsequence. This is equivalent to the open cover definition in metric spaces.
notes: Correct equivalence but defines via sequential compactness, not open covers

prompt: Using the formal definition explain why (0 1) is not compact.
learner_response: (0,1)은 open이므로 compact가 아니다. closed set만 compact가 될 수 있다.
notes: closed_implies_compact misconception (reversed logic)

prompt: Explain compactness from memory.
learner_response: Compact set: every open cover has a finite subcover. In R^n, equivalent to closed and bounded (Heine-Borel). Counterexample: (0,1) — cover by (1/n,1), no finite subcover. Proof of Heine-Borel uses nested intervals / bisection. Key misconception: bounded alone is not sufficient.
notes: Comprehensive recall
